framework.reasoning: DNAGPT is a CUSTOM GPT architecture (dual Sequential/Numerical embeddings, dual Classification/Regression heads, a bespoke token language, and a 3-task pre-training loss) whose contribution IS the architecture. Official weights are .pth files hosted on Google Drive / Tencent Weiyun (NOT the HuggingFace Hub) and are loaded via the authors' own model class in TencentAILabHealthcare/DNAGPT (test.py), so there is no AutoModel path. The reproduction therefore (a) re-implements the DNAGPT architecture + token language + combined loss from scratch in plain PyTorch so it is fully unit-testable on CPU (no GPU or custom kernels needed), and (b) provides an official-weights loading path (state_dict key-mapping) + a GSR fine-tuning harness for reproducing the paper's Table S2 numbers on a GPU. Pure PyTorch, no custom CUDA kernels, so cuda_required=false for the architecture/tests; GPU only speeds up fine-tuning.

module_hierarchy.description: The DNAGPT model: Sequential + Numerical embeddings -> GPT decoder stack (causal) -> Classification + Regression heads. From-scratch, CPU-runnable. Exposes forward(seq_ids, numbers) -> (class_logits, reg_values) and from_pretrained(path) for the official .pth.

public_methods.name: forward
public_methods.signature: forward(self, seq_ids: torch.Tensor, numbers: torch.Tensor = None, number_positions: torch.Tensor = None) -> Dict[str, torch.Tensor]

public_methods.name: from_pretrained
public_methods.signature: from_pretrained(cls, ckpt_path: str, config: DNAGPTConfig, device: str) -> 'DNAGPT'
public_methods.returns: model with official weights loaded (state_dict key-mapped)

module_hierarchy.description: GPT decoder block (causal masked multi-head self-attention + FFN + LayerNorm), Sequential/Numerical embedding modules, and the Classification/Regression heads.

public_methods.name: forward
public_methods.signature: forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, attn_mask: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor

module_hierarchy.description: The DNAGPT token language: non-overlapping k-mer sequence tokens (A/C/G/T/N), number tokens, instruction tokens (per-species + classification/number), connection tokens ('+','='), classification tokens ('A'/'N' = True/False), reserved tokens. Encodes the fine-tuning templates from Fig 2b.

public_methods.name: build_classification_example
public_methods.signature: build_classification_example(self, species: str, seq: str, label: bool) -> Dict
public_methods.returns: token ids for a GSR classification template

module_hierarchy.description: GSR (genomic signal & region) dataset loader following DeepGSR: PAS/TIS for human/mouse/bovine/fruit fly, 300bp flanks, motif removed, 6:1.5:2.5 split. Binary classification.
Combined pre-training loss L = lambda*MSE + CE (lambda=0.01), plus task-specific heads for GC regression, sequence-order CE, next-token CE.
GSR fine-tuning loop (AdamW, cosine+warmup, 10 epochs, lr = 1/3 pretrain, wd 1e-1, bs 8) with early stopping; classification head + CE.
ACC, F1, MCC, precision, recall (GSR); r2 (mRNA); Wasserstein + correlation helpers (generation).
YAML config loader + seeding + DNAGPT variant registry (H/M/S-512/B-512 dims) + GSR task registry.

tensor_flows.name: GSR classification forward
steps: non-overlap 6-mer tokenize DNA + prepend species instruction token -> seq_ids
GPT causal decoder x12 -> hidden [B, L, D]
take final-token hidden -> classification head -> logits [B, 2] (True/False via 'A'/'N' tokens)

tensor_flows.name: Combined pre-training step
steps: encode DNA (+ optional numbers) via token language
CE over sequence/order tokens + MSE over GC/number tokens

runtime.reason: core framework (from-scratch model, no custom kernels)
download official .pth checkpoints from Google Drive
parse genome FASTA / cDNA for GSR extraction

entrypoints.purpose: Fine-tune DNAGPT on a GSR recognition task
Evaluate a fine-tuned checkpoint on the GSR test split
Download official DNAGPT weights (Google Drive) and/or GSR datasets

risk_assessment.description: Official weights are on Google Drive / Weiyun (not HF Hub) and load via the authors' custom class; state_dict keys may not match a from-scratch re-implementation, so exact-weight reproduction of Table S2 needs a key-mapping step that must be validated against the real checkpoint.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Ship a from-scratch model whose module names mirror the paper; provide from_pretrained() with a documented key-map + strict=False fallback. The architecture + token language + loss are fully unit-tested on CPU regardless of weights; GSR fine-tuning from scratch is also runnable (lower accuracy) if weights can't be mapped.

risk_assessment.description: GSR data (DeepGSR PAS/TIS) requires downloading per-organism genomes/cDNA and reproducing the 300bp-flank + motif-removal preprocessing, which is nontrivial.
risk_assessment.mitigation: data/download.py + a documented extraction script; a small synthetic GSR generator lets the pipeline + tests run without the full download.

risk_assessment.description: Numerical embedding / regression head MLP shapes are unspecified (SIR conf 0.6).
risk_assessment.mitigation: Use a sensible 2-layer MLP with GELU; mark # ASSUMED; the sequence-classification path (GSR reproduction target) does not depend on these.

risk_assessment.description: AdamW 'momentum 0.937' in Fig S3 is unusual alongside betas.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Use standard AdamW betas (0.9, 0.98); ignore the momentum field with a # ASSUMED note.
